title: Failing to attach container to ipvlan network interface
body:
### Issue Description

Hi,

I was trying to create an ipvlan just like with docker but I got some weird and very cryptic errors like so.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
~ $ sudo podman network create -d ipvlan --subnet 192.168.88.0/24 --gateway 192.168.88.1 -o parent=eno1 testipvlan
testipvlan
~ $ sudo podman run --rm -ti --network=testipvlan --ip=192.168.88.37 fedora:latest bash
Error: netavark: create ipvlan interface: Netlink error: Operation not supported (os error 95)
```

### Describe the results you received

```
Error: netavark: create ipvlan interface: Netlink error: Operation not supported (os error 95)
```

### Describe the results you expected

Container attaching to the interface as expected

```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootful

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

It's possible there might be something missconfigured but I have no clue how to debug this further or figure it out with the errors provided.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Sounds like you are not allowed to create a ipvlan device, can you run `ip link add link <parent name> name ipvlan-test type ipvlan` on the host?

Indeed that's broken

```
sudo ip link add link eno1 name ipvlan-test type ipvlan
RTNETLINK answers: Operation not supported
```
I would guess you are missing the ipvlan kernel module, either way not a podman issue.
